focus: Q5 - Define routing heuristics for MiniMax 2.7 versus DeepSeek/Qwen/Kimi/GLM and consolidate patch-ready file-level deltas

graphEvents.label: Prefer MiniMax when minimax-api quota isolation matters
Prefer DeepSeek-v4-pro for hardest root-cause reasoning
Prefer Qwen3.6 for narrow bounded edits
Prefer Kimi-k2.6 for established long-context opencode-go runs
Prefer GLM-5.1 for structured broad synthesis

Q5 routing and Q1-Q4 findings produce patch-ready file deltas

label: MiniMax should be routed for large context and minimax-api quota isolation, not made the universal cli-opencode default
MiniMax highspeed is a distinct model-selection path; omit --variant by default until ablation proves a MiniMax mapping

Patch-ready deltas touch model-profiles, per-model-budgets, canonical and mirrored context-budget docs, cli_reference, pattern-index, prompt-quality cards, and permissions docs
Do not build MiniMax-specific fallback or permission systems; extend 114 registry, budget, verification, fallback, and permissions infrastructure
Keep minimax-2.7 fallback_target null until a verified separate-pool fallback is deliberately adopted
MiniMax docs list MiniMax-M2.7 and MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed with 204800-token context windows and different output speeds
OpenCode docs describe variants as provider/model-specific configuration and do not prove a built-in MiniMax --variant mapping

Add MiniMax-2.7 to the canonical per-model budget table and active-set wording; note active tool-call/tool-result rounds must remain intact when trimming.

Document MiniMax 204800 context, separate minimax-api pool, --variant omitted by default, highspeed as exact model id if exposed, routing heuristics table, and variant ablation recipe.

direction: Make MiniMax the universal cli-opencode default immediately
reason: Current evidence supports targeted routing for context and quota isolation, but live latency and quality benchmarks are still missing.
Official MiniMax docs expose highspeed as a separate model name, and OpenCode public docs do not prove a MiniMax-specific built-in variant mapping.

direction: Add MiniMax-specific fallback or permission logic
reason: 114 already owns provider-neutral fallback and permissions primitives; MiniMax should extend those registries and docs rather than duplicating runtime policy.
